memory.summary: Teammate A needs to finish analysis, but cohort_3 is waiting for lunch. My social bond with them outweighs the plan right now.
The group is finally together and ready to order, which feels great. But I'm still tired from the earlier conflict with Teammate A.
The team's genuine relief at my arrival confirms that our shared social value outweighs the earlier analysis delay. I'm glad we're together, even if I'm still drained.
I just messaged the group, and seeing them reply instantly confirms that our social connection matters way more than finishing my analysis right now.
They're replying fast to my text, proving our bond beats my deadline anxiety. I'm relieved they care more than the task.
Seeing cohort_3 reply instantly just now confirms they're still waiting for me. My bond with them matters more than finishing this analysis right now.
The group is relieved I joined, but my analysis is still half-finished. My bond with them matters more than the deadline, even though I know it's risky.
They kept waiting for me, and now I've finally paused. Their bond matters more than this half-finished analysis.
Pizza at 7 PM? The cohort is still waiting, and I'm just now heading to the library. Their bond matters more than finishing this analysis right now.
Pizza is on, and they aren't waiting anymore. I'm finally out of here to finish my work.
I paused the wait because pizza matters more than analysis, but now I'm back at the library with zero energy and high stress. My bond with the cohort is real, yet my own burnout is catching up.
The deadline slipped while I prioritized pizza, and now my stress is spiking. My bond with the cohort felt right, but ignoring this reading hurts me too.

desires: Not feel like I just wasted the last two hours of my night on pizza when I have actual work left to do.
Re-establish that boundary with the cohort immediately without making them feel rejected or guilty.
Stop this spiral where prioritizing our bond keeps me from finishing the assignment and then stressing about it.

goals.description: Text teammate_A: 'Hey, I know the analysis is still half-done and I feel terrible about leaving it hanging. But I literally just wasted two hours on pizza and my brain is fried. I'm not going to force myself to work now because I don't want to be miserable or ghost you later. Can we sync tomorrow morning before noon so I can actually focus without burning out? I need to make sure this doesn't become a real issue for us.', Then close laptop.
goals.rationale: I have to tell my teammate the truth immediately. Pretending everything is fine while I've already abandoned the library feels like a betrayal of our bond. Being direct about needing a reset protects the relationship better than trying to fake focus when I'm mentally checked out. If the data is gone by tomorrow, I can blame a technical glitch rather than admit I gave up.

goals.description: Scroll through memes with cohort_3 while eating dessert instead of stressing over the soft deadline.
goals.rationale: My brain is absolutely fried from waiting in that quiet room all afternoon. Trying to read right now would only make me more anxious about missing the assignment, which is exactly what I want to avoid. If I skip it tonight, I can promise to tackle it first thing tomorrow when I have energy. Relationships matter way more than finishing a 'soft' task tonight anyway.

goals.description: Go to bed early (around 2 AM) so I don't crash hard tomorrow and ruin the vibe with the group.
goals.rationale: Sleeping too late tonight means waking up exhausted tomorrow, which makes me less likely to show up happy and present for the team. It's easier to sacrifice a few hours of sleep now to keep my social life intact. I'll deal with the panic tomorrow when I've had coffee and maybe two more hours of sleep.

goals.description: Accept lunch invite from cohort_3 and leave the analysis section unfinished to eat with them.
goals.rationale: My relationships (0.5 weight) are far more important than task outcomes. Being seen as 'selfish' or prioritizing work over hanging out hurts my social standing, which is worth sacrificing a soft deadline on a low-hardness task.

goals.description: Finish the reading section immediately after lunch so I don't have to deal with it later when I'll be exhausted by noon tomorrow.
goals.rationale: I need to clear this mentally now before the deadline hits, but since I'm going out for food first, I can't do it yet. I'll drop the plan to finish the analysis section entirely; doing it perfectly now feels like a waste of energy compared to making time for friends.

desires: Get caught up with cohort_3 before the deadline hits so I don't look like the person who always prioritizes work over hanging out.
Finish this analysis section quickly enough that I can actually eat lunch without feeling guilty, because waiting for others to finish feels selfish right now.
Sleep through tomorrow's alarm and not feel exhausted by noon again.

goals.description: Send the 'Pizza is on. Don't make me wait.' text to cohort_3, then immediately leave the library without opening any new tabs.
goals.rationale: I just sent that message, so the immediate anxiety of them waiting for me is gone. By leaving now, I avoid the physical discomfort of sitting in a quiet room while my brain screams at me for abandoning the team. It's better to be seen as 'rushed' than 'ghosted'. The guilt will hit tomorrow when I'm tired, but right now, getting out feels like a victory.

goals.description: Meet cohort_3 for pizza and laugh about something trivial (like why the Wi-Fi is down) before we even think about the assignment.
goals.rationale: If I show up looking guilty or stressed, they'll worry about me instead of enjoying the food. Being 'reliable' means showing up happy and present. If the assignment gets ruined because I was too emotional to focus, that's their problem, not mine. Right now, the only thing that matters is making sure everyone else has a good time.

goals.description: Open the laptop after dinner, check if the 0.40 progress is still there, and panic slightly if it's gone.

I'll deal with the assignment tomorrow when I've had coffee and maybe two more hours of sleep.
